_notes: OPEC membership as of May 2026: 12 member states. Angola left at the end of 2023; Qatar left in 2019; Ecuador left in 2020; Indonesia suspended in 2016. The wider OPEC+ alliance (which includes Russia, Mexico, Kazakhstan and others) is a coordination forum, not OPEC membership, and is not included here.
name: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
mission: Cartel of major oil-producing states coordinating petroleum policy, particularly production quotas and pricing. Members control roughly 30% of world oil supply and 70% of proven reserves.
